title: 10 best herbal teas for jaw pain
intro_desc: Herbal teas for jaw pain are infusions made from various plants and herbs that can help alleviate discomfort and pain in the jaw area. These teas are often used as a natural alternative to over-the-counter pain relievers and can be particularly helpful for those who experience jaw pain due to conditions such as temporomandibular joint disorder (TMJ). Some examples of herbal teas that can help with jaw pain include chamomile, ginger, and turmeric teas. Chamomile tea has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling in the jaw area, while ginger and turmeric teas can help alleviate muscle tension and pain. By drinking these herbal teas, individuals can find relief from jaw pain and improve their overall quality of life, allowing them to enjoy daily activities without discomfort.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: White willow teas helps with jaw pain because of its natural analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties. The tea's active compound, salicin, is similar to aspirin and has been shown to reduce swelling and ease discomfort in the temporomandibular joint (TMJ). When consumed as a warm tea, white willow's soothing effects can help relax tense jaw muscles and calm inflammation, providing relief from common causes of jaw pain such as teeth grinding, TMJ disorders, or stress-induced bruxism.
remedy_properties: Salicin: White willow tea contains salicin, a natural compound similar to aspirin, which helps reduce inflammation and pain in the temporomandibular joint (TMJ) and surrounding tissues.

Anti-inflammatory flavonoids: The flavonoids present in white willow tea, such as quercetin and kaempferol, have potent anti-inflammatory effects that can help alleviate jaw pain and stiffness by reducing swelling and irritation.

Antioxidant activity: White willow tea's antioxidant properties help protect the jaw muscles and surrounding tissues from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to chronic pain and inflammation in the jaw.
remedy_parts: Bark: The bark contains salicin, a natural compound similar to aspirin, which helps to reduce inflammation and relieve pain in the jaw.
Roots: The roots of the white willow plant contain a higher concentration of salicin than other parts of the plant, making them effective for alleviating toothaches and jaw pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried white willow bark in quantities sufficient for your desired amount of tea.
Rinse the harvested white willow bark with cold filtered water to remove any dirt or debris.
Chop the white willow bark into small pieces and combine with 1 tablespoon of loose-leaf herbal tea for every 8 ounces of boiling water.
Steep the mixture for 10-15 minutes in a glass teapot or heat-resistant cup to allow flavors to meld.
Strain the tea using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a clean glass and discard solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sumac teas helps with jaw pain because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which effectively reduce swelling and discomfort in the jaw and surrounding muscles. The tea's antioxidant-rich compounds also help to relax tense muscles and ease stiffness, providing relief from temporomandibular joint (TMJ) disorders. Additionally, sumac's analgesic properties provide a soothing warmth that calms and reduces pain, allowing for a more comfortable chewing and speaking experience.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Sumac tea's antioxidant properties help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress, which can contribute to jaw pain conditions like temporomandibular joint (TMJ) disorder.
Pain-relieving compounds: Sumac contains compounds like quercetin, a flavonoid that has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which can help relieve jaw pain and discomfort.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Sumac tea's anti-inflammatory properties may help reduce swelling and inflammation in the jaw joint and surrounding tissues, reducing pressure and tension that can contribute to jaw pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the sumac plant are rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in reducing inflammation and pain associated with jaw pain.
Seeds: Sumac seeds have been traditionally used to treat various oral health issues, including toothaches and gum infections, which can contribute to jaw pain.
remedy_recipe: Measure out 2 teaspoons of dried sumac flowers and leaves per cup.
Bring 1 cup of pure water to a boil in a medium-sized saucepan.
Steep the sumac mixture in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes to release flavors.
Strain the tea into a teapot or individual cups using a fine-mesh sieve.
Serve the tea hot, sweetening with honey if desired, and enjoy within 24 hours.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice teas helps with jaw pain because it contains glycyrrhizin, a compound that has anti-inflammatory properties. When consumed as a tea, it can help reduce inflammation in the temporomandibular joint (TMJ) and surrounding tissues, which is often the source of chronic jaw pain. Additionally, licorice tea's soothing properties can also calm irritated muscles and joints, providing relief from tension headaches and migraines that may be exacerbated by TMJ disorders. As a result, herbal licorice teas have been traditionally used to alleviate symptoms of temporomandibular joint dysfunction (TMD) and promote overall oral health.
remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: Licorice root contains glycyrrhizin, which has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that help reduce swelling and ease pain in the jaw and surrounding areas, providing relief from conditions like temporomandibular joint (TMJ) disorder.

Flavonoids: Herbal licorice tea contains flavonoids, such as quercetin and luteolin, which have potent anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation and alleviate pain in the jaw and face, making it an effective natural remedy for migraines and headaches.

Glycyrrhizic acid: Licorice root's glycyrrhizic acid has been shown to have a direct relaxing effect on muscle tissue, which can help ease tension in the jaw muscles and reduce grinding and clenching, a common cause of jaw pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in glycyrrhizin, a compound that has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, making it effective in relieving jaw pain and reducing inflammation.
Bark: Contains flavonoids and phenolic acids that have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which help to reduce pain and swelling associated with jaw pain.
remedy_recipe: Measure 2 tablespoons of dried licorice root into a heat-resistant glass cup.
Pour 8 ounces of boiling water over the licorice root and let it steep for 10 minutes.
Strain the tea using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove the solids.
Add honey to taste if desired and stir well before serving immediately.
Store any leftover tea in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 24 hours.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: German chamomile teas helps with jaw pain because its soothing and calming properties can ease muscle tension and inflammation in the face and jaw. The tea's apigenin content, a flavonoid with anti-inflammatory effects, helps to reduce swelling and discomfort in the temporomandibular joint (TMJ), while its calming effect on the nervous system can alleviate stress and anxiety, common triggers of jaw clenching and grinding.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: German Chamomile tea has anti-inflammatory compounds like apigenin, luteolin, and bisabolol, which can help reduce inflammation in the jaw muscles and joints, alleviating pain and discomfort.

Anxiolytic (anxiety-reducing) properties: The tea's flavonoids and terpenes have a calming effect on the nervous system, helping to ease tension and stress that may contribute to jaw pain and clenching.

Sedative and relaxing properties: German Chamomile's sedative compounds like apigenin can help promote relaxation, reduce muscle spasms, and calm the mind and body, all of which can help alleviate jaw pain and related symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in apigenin, a flavonoid that helps relax muscles and reduce inflammation, making them effective for alleviating jaw pain.
Flowers: Contain anti-inflammatory compounds like bisabolol and azulene, which soothe the gums and reduce swelling associated with jaw pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 teaspoons of dried german chamomile flowers for every 8 ounces of water needed.
Heat 8 ounces of boiling water in a teapot or kettle for 3-5 minutes.
Add the dried german chamomile flowers to the hot water and steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove the solids.
Enjoy the soothing and calming herbal tea within 30 minutes of preparation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Common lime teas helps with jaw pain because it contains a natural anti-inflammatory property that reduces swelling and inflammation in the temporomandibular joint (TMJ). The tea's calming effects also help to relax the muscles of the face and jaw, which can become strained and tense from conditions such as teeth grinding or clenching. Additionally, the lime tea's soothing warmth helps to ease stiffness and discomfort in the jaw, providing relief from tension headaches and migraines often associated with TMJ disorders.
remedy_properties: Antioxidants: Common lime tea contains antioxidants like flavonoids, phenolic acids, and ascorbic acid that help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the body, which can contribute to jaw pain.
Anti-inflammatory compounds: The tea contains anti-inflammatory compounds like citric acid, malic acid, and limonene, which can help reduce swelling and ease pain in the jaw muscles and joints.
Mucilages: Common lime tea also contains mucilages like pectin and hemicellulose, which can help soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the mouth and throat, reducing inflammation and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: Roots are a good source of calcium and magnesium, which can help relax muscles and reduce tension in the jaw.
Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes contain compounds that have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate jaw pain caused by inflammation or arthritis.
remedy_recipe: Gather one tablespoon of dried tilia europaea flowers per cup of boiling water.
Heat fresh filtered water in a pot or kettle until it reaches 212°f temperature.
Pour the hot water over the dried flower mixture and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea leaves using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a cup.
Add one teaspoon of honey per cup of tea to sweeten the flavor as desired.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Large-leaved lime teas helps with jaw pain because its unique blend of antioxidants, flavonoids, and other bioactive compounds work synergistically to reduce inflammation and relax muscles. The tea's anti-inflammatory properties help alleviate swollen tissues and joints associated with temporomandibular joint disorders (TMJ), while its calming effects ease tension in the jaw muscles, allowing for smoother movements and reduced discomfort.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Large-leaved lime tea contains antioxidants like flavonoids and phenolic acids, which help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the body, thereby alleviating jaw pain caused by conditions like temporomandibular joint (TMJ) disorder.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The tea's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as limonoids and alkaloids, help reduce swelling and discomfort in the jaw muscles and joints, providing relief from tension headaches and facial pain associated with jaw problems.

Muscle relaxant properties: Large-leaved lime tea contains muscle-relaxing compounds like citral and geraniol, which help calm tense muscles in the face, neck, and jaw, reducing pain and discomfort caused by bruxism, teeth grinding, or other oral habits.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the large-leaved lime plant contain compounds that have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing swelling and pain associated with jaw pain.
Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes, the underground stems, have analgesic properties that help alleviate pain and discomfort caused by jaw pain, while also soothing inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh leaves of tilia platyphyllos, selecting those that are bright green and free from damage.
Rinse the harvested leaves with cool running water, gently removing any dirt or debris.
Combine one teaspoon of dried or two teaspoons of fresh tilia platyphyllos leaves with 250ml boiling water in a heat-resistant teapot.
Steep the tea mixture for seven to ten minutes, allowing the herbal flavors and properties to infuse into the hot water.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth, discarding the solids and serving the liquid immediately.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Littleleaf linden teas helps with jaw pain because of its calming and anti-inflammatory properties. The tea's active compounds, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, work together to relax the muscles and reduce swelling in the jaw joint, providing relief from tension headaches, TMJ disorders, and teeth grinding. Additionally, littleleaf linden's soothing effects can help alleviate stress and anxiety, common triggers of bruxism and other oral health issues that contribute to jaw pain.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Herbal littleleaf linden teas contain compounds that reduce inflammation, which can contribute to jaw pain (e.g., temporomandibular joint disorder) by decreasing swelling and alleviating pressure on the joint.

Antioxidant: The antioxidants present in littleleaf linden tea help protect the body from free radicals that can damage tissues and cells, including those in the jaw, reducing oxidative stress that may contribute to pain.

Muscle relaxant: Littleleaf linden tea's flavonoids and other compounds have been shown to relax muscles, which can be beneficial in easing tension headaches and migraines that often accompany jaw pain, as well as providing relief from clenched jaws and facial spasms.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the littleleaf linden plant have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing swelling and pain associated with jaw disorders.

Bark: The bark of the littleleaf linden plant contains salicin, a compound similar to aspirin, which helps to alleviate tension headaches and jaw pain caused by temporomandibular joint (TMJ) disorder.
remedy_recipe: Gather fresh or dried tilia cordata leaves and flowers from sustainable sources.
Measure out one teaspoon of dried linden leaves and flowers for every 8 ounces of boiling water.
Combine the measured linden herb with boiling water in a teapot or heat-resistant cup.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes to allow the herbal properties to infuse into the liquid.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or tea strainer and enjoy warm or chilled.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm teas helps with jaw pain because it has a calming effect on the muscles of the face, particularly the masseter and temporalis muscles that are responsible for jaw clenching. The tea's anti-inflammatory properties also help to reduce swelling and discomfort in the joints of the jaw, known as temporomandibular joint (TMJ) disorder. This natural relaxation can alleviate tension and pain caused by teeth grinding, bruxism, or other jaw-related issues.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic properties: Lemon balm tea helps to relax muscle spasms, which can contribute to tension headaches and jaw pain. This relaxation of muscles in the face and neck can provide relief from painful contractions.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Lemon balm contains compounds that have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce swelling and inflammation in the jaw joint (TMJ) that may be contributing to pain and discomfort.

Anxiolytic and sedative properties: Lemon balm tea has a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help alleviate stress, anxiety, and tension, common contributors to jaw pain and teeth grinding (bruxism).
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in calcium and magnesium, lemon balm roots help relax jaw muscles and reduce tension, easing jaw pain.
Bark: Containing compounds like rosmarinic acid, lemon balm bark helps reduce inflammation and soothe the joints, relieving jaw pain.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2-3 tablespoons of dried melissa officinalis leaves and flowers.
Heat one cup of filtered water in a saucepan to boiling point within five minutes.
Steep the herb mixture in the boiling water for ten to fifteen minutes carefully.
Strain the tea mixture through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a teapot promptly.
Pour the strained lemon balm tea into cups and serve hot immediately.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint teas helps with jaw pain because of its natural ability to relax the muscles and ease tension. The menthol in peppermint has a calming effect on the temporomandibular joint (TMJ), reducing inflammation and spasms that can cause jaw pain, such as migraines and headaches. Additionally, the tea's cooling sensation can help numb the pain, providing quick relief from discomfort and stiffness. By drinking herbal peppermint tea regularly, individuals may experience a significant reduction in jaw pain and improved overall oral health.
remedy_properties: Analgesic and anti-inflammatory compounds: Peppermint tea contains analgesics like menthol, which can help numb the pain in the jaw muscles and reduce inflammation, providing relief from conditions such as temporomandibular joint (TMJ) disorder.

Antispasmodic properties: The menthol and other compounds in peppermint tea have been shown to relax muscle spasms and reduce contractions, which can help alleviate tension headaches and jaw pain caused by bruxism or teeth grinding.

Digestive benefits: Peppermint tea has been known to soothe digestive issues such as IBS and acid reflux, which are often linked to jaw pain, as a healthy gut microbiome is essential for overall oral health.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the peppermint plant have a calming effect on the muscles and nerves, making them effective in soothing jaw pain caused by tension or inflammation.
Rhyzomes: Peppermint rhizomes contain analgesic compounds that help reduce pain and inflammation in the temporomandibular joint (TMJ), alleviating jaw pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh peppermint leaves or use dried peppermint flowers at a ratio of one teaspoon per cup.
Rinse the peppermint leaves with cold water to remove any dirt or debris and pat them dry with paper towels.
Combine the peppermint leaves with 200ml of boiling water in a teapot or infuser for a steeping time of 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a cup or teapot and discard the solids.
Add honey or lemon to taste and serve hot or chilled as desired.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Spearmint teas helps with jaw pain because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which can reduce swelling and alleviate tension in the jaw muscles. The calming effect of spearmint's essential oils also soothes the nervous system, helping to relax the jaw and facial muscles that contribute to chronic pain. As a result, regular consumption of herbal spearmint teas may provide relief from temporomandibular joint (TMJ) disorders, headaches, and other forms of jaw discomfort.
remedy_properties: Eugenol: Spearmint tea contains eugenol, a natural analgesic and anti-inflammatory compound that helps to reduce swelling and alleviate pain in the jaw, making it an effective treatment for temporomandibular joint (TMJ) disorder.

Menthol: Menthol, another key component of spearmint tea, has a cooling effect on the muscles and joints, which can help to relax tense facial and jaw muscles that contribute to jaw pain and discomfort.

Flavonoids: Spearmint tea contains flavonoids, such as quercetin, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help to reduce inflammation in the jaw joint and surrounding tissues, thereby alleviating pain and stiffness.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the spearmint plant contain compounds like menthol and methyleugenol, which can help reduce inflammation and relax muscles in the jaw, providing relief from jaw pain.
Bark: The bark of the spearmint plant contains flavonoids and phenolic acids that have anti-inflammatory properties, making it effective in reducing swelling and pain associated with jaw disorders.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried spearmint leaves from your garden or store them in an airtight container for up to one year.
Measure out one teaspoon of dried spearmint leaves or two teaspoons of fresh leaves per eight-ounce cup of boiling water.
Heat filtered water to a rolling boil and pour it over the measured spearmint leaves in a teapot or infuser.
Allow the mixture to steep for five to seven minutes, depending on your desired strength of mint flavor.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth and discard the solids before serving immediately.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat jaw pain with herbal teas is by choosing teas with anti-inflammatory properties, such as chamomile, ginger, or clove. Steep the tea in hot water for several minutes, then let it cool down to a comfortable temperature. Sip the tea slowly, allowing it to gently bathe the affected area. The anti-inflammatory properties of these herbs can help reduce swelling and alleviate pain. For best results, drink several cups of herbal tea throughout the day. However, if your jaw pain persists or worsens, it is important to seek medical attention.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to jaw pain that are treated with herbal teas include temporomandibular joint (TMJ) disorder and bruxism. TMJ disorder can cause pain and discomfort in the jaw joint and muscles, while bruxism is characterized by teeth grinding or clenching, often resulting in jaw pain and headaches. Herbal teas such as chamomile, valerian root, and passionflower are commonly used to alleviate symptoms of these conditions due to their anti-inflammatory, muscle relaxant, and calming properties. However, it is essential to consult with a healthcare professional for proper diagnosis and treatment.
